title: Fixing GIT Branch Name Validation for Invalid Unicode Sequences

statement.markdown: Write a function to validate GIT branch names. It should return `true` if the branch name is valid and `false` otherwise. The branch name is considered valid if it is not empty and contains only alphanumeric characters and hyphens. Additionally, the branch name should not be a reserved name (e.g., 'HEAD', 'master', 'dev', etc.).
statement.inputFormat: The input will be a string representing the branch name.
statement.outputFormat: The function should return a boolean indicating whether the branch name is valid or not.
statement.notes: The function should handle reserved names case-insensitively.

constraints.description: Number of reserved names to check against
Length of the branch name to validate

examples.explanation: The branch name 'master' is a reserved name.
The branch name 'HEAD' is a reserved name.

hints.text: Check the branch name against the reserved names list in a case-insensitive manner.

editorial.title: Fixing GIT Branch Name Validation
editorial.intuition: The key to solving this problem is to handle the reserved names case-insensitively.
editorial.bruteForce: One approach to solve this problem is to iterate through all possible branch names and check if they are valid.
editorial.betterSolution: A more efficient solution would be to use a regular expression to match valid branch names.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution would be to use a set data structure to store the reserved names and check if the branch name is in the set in a case-insensitive manner.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The proof of correctness involves showing that the function returns the correct result for all possible inputs.
commonMistakes: Mistaking the branch name as a reserved name.
Not handling the reserved names case-insensitively.
